title: pelargonium burtoniae
intro: Pelargonium burtoniae, commonly known as the 'Lemon Rose', is a succulent plant species native to South Africa, belonging to the Geranium family. The plant's leaves and stems have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Pelargonium burtoniae are said to offer various health benefits, including relief from colds, fever, and digestive issues, as well as helping to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant is often infused in hot water or made into a tea, and its essential oil is extracted and used in aromatherapy and skincare products.
